questions.question: How would you improve the decision time for users when ordering from food delivery apps like Swiggy?
As a PM at Swiggy, if you observed a 20% decrease in orders last week, how would you conduct a Root Cause Analysis?
Should Zomato allow users to order from multiple restaurants in a single order? If so, how would you implement it? If not, why?
How would you understand why the lunch conversion rate is low (e.g., 2% compared to 10% for dinner and breakfast)?
Assume Flipkart wants to enter the food delivery business. How would you integrate food delivery into the current Flipkart app?
Design a lost and retrieval system for Ola Cabs.
How do you define a product vision for a payment API at Razorpay, and what steps do you take to ensure it aligns with business objectives?
Design a fitness app for Meta.
Define success metrics for TikTok (or Reels).
How would you increase customer lifetime value for Swiggy?
Improve Facebook (or any core social feature).
If Amazon were losing 10% of its revenue monthly, what metrics would you look into?
Design an app for an amusement park.
What’s your favorite Microsoft product and how would you improve it?
Tell me about yourself.
Why Microsoft?
Why do you want to do product management?
Why do you want this job, and what can you bring that we don't already have?
Explain the PM position to someone who doesn't get it.
Tell me about a time when you had to work through a problem with a teammate.
Tell me about a time when you faced conflict within a team and how you dealt with it.
Tell me about a product you led from idea to launch.
Tell me about the most challenging product you’ve worked on.
Tell me about a time you failed.
Tell me about big-budget projects you’ve worked on. How do you ensure that you work within budget?
What’s the biggest risk you’ve taken in managing a project?
How do you know what your customers want?
How do you get people to agree with your point of view?
How can a product manager influence without being authoritative?
If you want to get something built, how will you influence the developers to build it and the senior leadership to agree with this idea?
In working with teams, what is an achievement that you are most proud of?
How do you manage a project where you need to scale up and then scale down frequently?
How do you keep track of multiple tasks?
Design an elevator system.
How would you design an alarm clock for the hard of hearing?
How would you design a washing machine?
Design a 3-button remote for a television.
Design a vending machine to be located at an airport gate.
Design a kitchen.
Design a microwave oven for college students.
Design a product for campus recruitment.
Thinking of a product you’ve started using a lot lately and considering how to improve the user experience, draw out a new UI/UX.
Design the UI/UX for Disney’s new streaming platform.
Design the most engaging user interface for a music app that you can think of.
How would you design a Microsoft cell phone?
A new building owner wants an elevator to be constructed. What are some of the questions you will ask them or the factors you will consider before you give them the final recommendation?
If you were to design an app for selling and buying antiques, how would you do it?
What’s your favorite product, and how would you improve it?
How would you improve a past project you worked on?
Choose a popular product in the market. How would you improve it?
What’s your favorite product, and why? How would you improve it?
How would you improve a specific product line if the goal is to acquire new users?
Choose a relevant app you use. What would you improve? Why?
How would you add food ordering to Excel?
Imagine you’re a PM at a startup that works with big data. Now what?
If you were a PM on Microsoft Surface and Delta asked you to design the perfect airline entertainment system, what would you do? Walk through your thoughts and pitch it.
How would you monetize Outlook?
Take any production chain; how can you optimize the production process?
How would you motivate users to use your app every single day for a month?
You are the king of an island. You want to switch which side of the road everyone is driving on. How do you go about doing it?
How would you define a product strategy for a new AI-powered productivity tool?
Choose a target customer to build an MLP (minimum loveable product) for. Brainstorm multiple features to add to this to scale the product over time.
How would you assess whether or not to discontinue a product?
How would you explain Cloud to your grandmother?
How would you explain recursion to somebody who is not technical?
What product KPIs have you used, and how did they help you in managing products?
What are the key metrics to track at each stage of a product's lifecycle?
How would you conduct a competitive analysis for a new cloud-based gaming service?
Describe a time when you had to make a difficult decision based on incomplete data. How did you approach the situation?
How would you track user engagement in an app, and what KPIs would you use to improve it?
What metrics would you use to measure success for Microsoft Outlook?
How would you set goals for a Microsoft product of your choice?
For products like Windows, there are millions of customers; how do you test for all?
How much money do Americans spend on cat food every year?
How would you go about estimating the number of gas stations in the USA?
How would you weigh a plane without a scale, but other tools at your disposal?
There are 24 holes in the key. How many combinations of passwords can you think of?
Describe your product framework for prioritization.
How do you prioritize work items?
How would you prioritize features for a product with limited resources and a tight deadline?
How would you improve Google Maps?
Calculate the number of queries answered by Google per second.
What is your favorite product? Why?
How would you measure the success of Facebook Likes?
How do you say no to feature ideas or requests?
How would you approach the task of developing a new product within Amazon’s existing ecosystem?
Can you discuss a time where you had to make a critical decision about a product’s life cycle?
What strategies would you use to manage stakeholder expectations in a high-stakes project?
Given your understanding of Amazon’s customer base, how would you prioritize features for a new product launch?
Suppose there is a conflict between engineering and marketing teams on a product feature, how would you resolve it?
Please explain how you have used data to drive product development decisions.
Describe a scenario where you led a cross-functional team in launching a product.
Based on what you know about Amazon’s operations, how would you handle a product recall situation?
If a key product is underperforming, how would you identify the root cause and propose solutions?
Can you describe a time when you successfully managed a product backlog?
How would you ensure that a newly launched product aligns with Amazon’s brand identity and values?
How do you stay updated with market trends and consumer needs relevant to Amazon’s offerings?
With respect to Amazon’s global reach, can you talk about an experience managing a product in different markets?
Discuss a time when you had to pivot a product strategy due to unforeseen circumstances.
Imagine one of our products received negative feedback from customers, how would you incorporate this feedback into future iterations?
Can you outline your process for conducting competitive analysis in relation to Amazon’s position in the market?
Explain how you would collaborate with UX/UI designers to enhance user experience on an Amazon product.
Share an instance where you utilized A/B testing to improve a product’s performance.
How would you balance resource constraints while ensuring product quality at Amazon?
Talk about a time when you faced a significant product-related challenge and how you overcame it.
How do you approach risk management when overseeing the development of new products?
Can you share an example where you had to manage a trade-off between product functionality and cost?
A metric for a video streaming service dropped by 80%. What do you do?
Your new feature boosts Amazon Search by 10%, adds 2s to load time. What do you do?
How would you improve Amazon?
How would you launch (roll out) Amazon Go?

Evaluate the upsides and downsides of building a super app — an app having all major B2C features including entertainment, e-commerce, food ordering, hotel booking, cab booking, chat, holiday planning, gaming, med ordering, service booking, etc.
How would you determine if a specific block in your neighborhood is suitable for a new grocery store?
How would you sell live plants on Amazon?
You are a new PM at Google Pay. Design a feature for budgeting.
Imagine you are a PM in Amazon. How would you reduce the rate of returns?
For a retail store, users are frequently complaining about long queues during check out. What strategy would you use to improve the experience in the short term and long term?
What is the market size of toilet paper in the US?

Tell me about a time you were trying to understand a problem on your team and you had to go down several layers to figure it out. Who did you talk with and what information proved most valuable? How did you use that information to help solve the problem?

Amazon is developing a machine learning algorithm to better target shoppers who have saved items in their shopping carts. The algorithm will send an email to users to remind them about their shopping carts. What factors would you consider when designing this algorithm? What variables would you tweak to yield higher shopping cart purchase rates?
Explain some new Alexa Skillsets you would roll out and why.
Why should Amazon not hire you?
Design a simple load balancer for Google.com. What data structures would you use?
Walk me through a product you’ve built and launched.
How do you prioritize features when stakeholders disagree?
How would you design a new shopping experience for Prime members?
How would you validate an idea for a new Amazon device before building it?
How would you improve the Alexa experience for households with multiple users?
If Amazon wanted to expand further into physical retail, what problem would you solve first?
How do you decide what not to build?
How would you forecast next quarter’s revenue for a major Amazon product line?
How would you determine the success of a new feature on Amazon Prime Video?
What metrics would you track to evaluate the effectiveness of Amazon’s recommendation engine?
How would you identify and fix a drop in conversion rate after a major site redesign?
Which products should Amazon discount during Prime Day to maximize profit?
Why do you want to work at Amazon, and what makes you a good fit?
Tell me about a time you made a difficult prioritization decision.
Describe a time you disagreed with a stakeholder. How did you resolve it?
Talk about a time you failed and what you learned from it.
Tell me about a time you demonstrated ownership beyond your role.
What would your current manager say about you? What constructive criticisms might he give?
What are your three biggest strengths and weaknesses you have identified in yourself?
Tell me about a time when you went above and beyond to meet a customer’s needs.
Tell me about a time when you took full responsibility for a product’s success or failure.
Describe a project where you set a goal and met it. What steps did you take to ensure success?
Can you give an example of when you simplified a complex problem for your team?
Tell me about a time when you helped someone on your team grow in their role.
Tell me about a time you had to dive deep into data to solve a problem.
Describe a situation where you disagreed with a decision but committed to it for the team’s success.
Tell me about a time when you helped improve your team’s environment or culture.
Give an example of a time when you delivered results with limited resources.
Can you share an example of a project where you set a target and achieved it? What did you do to ensure success?
Tell me about a time you demonstrated customer obsession in a project.
How would you improve a product based on customer feedback?
Explain a time when you had to scale a product.
How would you measure customer satisfaction for a new feature?
How would you improve Amazon Prime Video?
Describe a product design challenge you’ve faced.
Tell me about a time you made a decision that affected multiple teams.
How do you ensure your decisions are based on data?
Tell me about a time you demonstrated customer obsession.
Give an example of when you had to make a calculated risk in a product decision.
How would you improve Amazon’s checkout process?
How would you design a new feature for Amazon Prime Video?
Explain how you would design a scalable web service architecture for a new Amazon product.
What was the strategic rationale behind Facebook's rebranding to Meta, and what product opportunities does this open up for the company?

Meta envisions the metaverse as a 'physicalized internet' where users can experience rather than just watch. Describe a new product or feature you would design for this metaverse that doesn't exist on current platforms, outlining its core functionality and user benefits.

Meta plans to sell its devices at cost or subsidized and offer developer services with low fees to foster an open and accessible metaverse economy. What potential business challenges and risks do you foresee with this strategy, and how would you mitigate them?

Privacy, safety, open standards, and interoperability are highlighted as crucial for the metaverse from day one. As a Product Manager, how would you ensure these principles are embedded into the core design and development of a new metaverse product?

Meta's mission remains 'to bring people together,' and the metaverse is the next frontier for connection. How would you evolve an existing Meta product (e.g., Instagram, WhatsApp, Messenger) to be 'metaverse-first' and enhance social connection within this new paradigm?
